plains-of-moab.name: Plains of Moab
plains-of-moab.modernName: Jordan Valley, east of Jordan River near Dead Sea
plains-of-moab.description: The plains east of the Jordan River opposite Jericho, where Moses addressed Israel before entering Canaan.

mount-sinai.name: Mount Sinai
mount-sinai.description: The mountain where God gave the Law to Moses. Also called Horeb in Deuteronomy.

mount-seir.name: Mount Seir
mount-seir.description: Mountain range in the land of Edom, territory of the descendants of Esau.

kadesh-barnea.name: Kadesh-barnea
kadesh-barnea.description: An oasis settlement where Israel encamped and from which the twelve spies were sent into Canaan.

bashan-ashtaroth.name: Ashtaroth (Bashan)
bashan-ashtaroth.description: A city in Bashan, one of the royal cities of Og king of Bashan.

arabah.name: Arabah
arabah.description: The great rift valley extending from the Sea of Galilee to the Gulf of Aqaba.

hill-country-canaan.name: Hill Country of Canaan
hill-country-canaan.description: The mountainous interior of Canaan, inhabited by the Amorites, which Israel was commanded to conquer.

negev.name: Negev
negev.description: The arid region in the south of Canaan, part of the land promised to Israel.

coastal-plain.name: Coastal Plain
coastal-plain.description: The low-lying region along the Mediterranean coast, part of the promised land described in Deuteronomy 1.

lebanon.name: Lebanon
lebanon.description: The northern mountain range marking the extent of the promised land.

euphrates-river.name: Euphrates River
euphrates-river.description: The great river forming the northeastern boundary of the promised land as described by God to Moses.

land-of-canaan.name: Land of Canaan
land-of-canaan.description: The land promised by God to the descendants of Abraham, which Israel was preparing to enter.

wilderness-of-paran.name: Wilderness of Paran
wilderness-of-paran.description: A large desert region in the Sinai Peninsula through which Israel traveled from Sinai to Kadesh-barnea.

valley-of-eshcol.name: Valley of Eshcol
valley-of-eshcol.description: The valley near Hebron where the spies cut a cluster of grapes as evidence of the land's fertility.

hill-country-kadesh.name: Hill Country near Kadesh
altNames: Hill Country of the Amorites near Kadesh
hill-country-kadesh.modernName: Hill region near Kadesh-barnea, northeastern Sinai
hill-country-kadesh.description: The hilly terrain near Kadesh-barnea where the Israelites were defeated after disobeying God's command.

hormah.name: Hormah
hormah.description: A settlement in the Negev where the Israelites were pursued and defeated by the Amorites after their disobedient attempt to enter Canaan.

seir-edom.name: Seir (Edom)
seir-edom.description: The region south of the Dead Sea belonging to the descendants of Esau, through which Israel traveled.

edrei.name: Edrei
edrei.description: A city in Bashan where Israel defeated Og, king of Bashan, as referenced in the introduction to Deuteronomy.

hill-country-hebron.name: Hill Country near Hebron
hill-country-hebron.description: The hill country near Hebron where the spies explored the land of Canaan.

wilderness-red-sea.name: Wilderness toward the Red Sea
wilderness-red-sea.description: The wilderness route leading toward the Red Sea (Gulf of Aqaba), where Israel wandered after the rebellion at Kadesh-barnea.

amorite-territory.name: Amorite Territory
amorite-territory.description: The hill country inhabited by the Amorites, where Israel was defeated after disobeying God at Kadesh-barnea.

jerusalem.name: Jerusalem
jerusalem.description: The capital city of the kingdom of Judah, site of Solomon's Temple, and the spiritual center of Israel. Called the 'strong city' in Isaiah's song of praise.

land-of-judah.name: Land of Judah
land-of-judah.description: The territory of the tribe and kingdom of Judah, stretching from the Negev to just north of Jerusalem, including the hill country, the Shephelah, and the wilderness.

dibon-moab.name: Dibon
dibon-moab.description: The capital city of Moab, east of the Dead Sea. In Isaiah's prophecy, Moab represents the 'lofty city' that God brings low in contrast to Jerusalem, the 'strong city' of his people.

nineveh.name: Nineveh
nineveh.description: The great capital of the Assyrian Empire on the Tigris River. Assyria was the dominant foreign power during Isaiah's ministry, and the 'other lords' who had dominion over Israel.

babylon.name: Babylon
babylon.description: The great city on the Euphrates, capital of Nebuchadnezzar's empire, and the prophetic 'Mystery Babylon' of Revelation 17-18 — the lofty city that God brings down to the dust.

caesarea-philippi.name: Caesarea Philippi
caesarea-philippi.description: A city at the southern foot of Mount Hermon, near the headwaters of the Jordan River. Built around a grotto sacred to the Greek god Pan, it was rebuilt by Herod Philip and named for Caesar. Here Peter confessed, 'Thou art the Christ, the Son of the living God,' and Jesus answered, 'Upon this rock I will build my church.'

magadan.name: Magadan
magadan.description: A fishing village on the western shore of the Sea of Galilee, the home region of Mary Magdalene. Jesus came here after feeding the four thousand (Matt 15:39) and was met by Pharisees and Sadducees seeking a sign from heaven.

sea-of-galilee.name: Sea of Galilee
sea-of-galilee.description: A freshwater lake in the lowlands of Galilee, the setting for much of Jesus' ministry. The disciples crossed its waters with Jesus, who warned them aboard the boat to beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees.

bethsaida.name: Bethsaida
bethsaida.modernName: et-Tell / el-Araj, north of the Sea of Galilee
bethsaida.description: A fishing village on the north shore of the Sea of Galilee, just east of where the Jordan enters the lake. Hometown of Peter, Andrew, and Philip. After the Pharisees demanded a sign at Magadan, Jesus and the disciples crossed back over to this 'other side' (Mark 8:22), and from here Jesus led them north to the coasts of Caesarea Philippi.

mount-hermon.name: Mount Hermon
mount-hermon.modernName: Jebel esh-Sheikh, Lebanon / Syria / Golan Heights border
mount-hermon.description: The highest mountain in the region (~2,814 m), snow-capped much of the year, rising just north of Caesarea Philippi. Six days after Peter's confession (Matt 16:13-20), Jesus took Peter, James, and John 'up into an high mountain apart' — traditionally identified with Hermon — where he was transfigured before them and Moses and Elijah appeared. At the foot of the mountain he healed the demon-possessed boy.

capernaum.name: Capernaum
capernaum.modernName: Kfar Nahum, north shore of the Sea of Galilee
capernaum.description: A fishing town on the north-west shore of the Sea of Galilee that became Jesus' ministry headquarters after he left Nazareth (Matt 4:13). Home of Peter and Andrew; site of the synagogue where Jesus often taught and of the temple-tax episode where Peter found the coin in the fish's mouth (Matt 17:24-27).

great-sea.name: The Great Sea
great-sea.description: The vast sea bordering the land of Israel to the west, called 'the great sea' in the Old Testament (Num 34:6; Josh 1:4). In Isaiah 27:1 the LORD slays 'the dragon that is in the sea' — leviathan, the symbol of chaos and the world-power set against God.
